label: false-dup
a: Integration tests clean up database state by running inside a transaction that is rolled back at the end of each test. Each test starts with a clean slate.
b: Unit tests mock all service interfaces and external dependencies. No real database connections, no real HTTP calls. Fast execution, complete isolation. Integration tests (a separate suite) use real dependencies.
reviewer_notes: Different test classes: A is integration tests using transaction rollback; B is unit tests with mocked dependencies. Different isolation strategies.
